forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the Bight will continue to move southeastwards to be centred southeast of Tasmania by Sunday allowing a trough over WA to link to a weak front south of the Bight moving over western and southern parts of SA later Sunday into Monday. Another high pressure system is expected to follow early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Dry apart from isolated showers about the southwest and possible thunderstorms about the far southwest. Maximum temperatures near average to slightly below average about the southeast, grading to above average to the north and west. Generally southeast to northeasterly winds and locally fresh afternoon sea breezes.
Dry apart from patchy showers in the southwest. South to southeasterly winds and locally fresh afternoon sea breezes.

Areas of high cloud, isolated showers and possible thunderstorms about the southwest, extending across the west, reaching the southern agricultural area later. Sunny in the far north. Maximum temperatures above average. Southeast to easterly winds in the northeast. Northeasterly winds otherwise, ahead of a southerly change in the southwest during the evening.

High cloud and isolated showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about eastern parts clearing to the east in the morning. Possible showers to follow about southern coasts and the Lower South East. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures above average. North to northwesterly winds ahead of a southwest to southerly change in the west and south, extending inland during the afternoon and evening.

text.p: Light showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about southern coasts and ranges during the morning. High cloud and isolated showers about the southwest, possibly reaching central parts in the evening. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures near average to slightly below average about the agricultural area, grading to above average in the north. Northerly winds in the north at first, with southwest to southeasterly winds extending throughout during the morning.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: Less than 2mm, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about southern coasts and the Nullarbor Plain.
